description: Build a gravity model of inter-zone spatial interactions for San Francisco using the UrbanSim dataset located at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5. Create a fully executed Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/gravity_model.ipynb using the 'UrbanSim (Python 3)' kernel. The model must link households and jobs to zones via buildings and parcels to calculate zone population (sum of 'persons') and zone employment (count of jobs). Calculate zone centroids as the mean x,y coordinates of parcels. Restrict the analysis to zones with population > 0, employment > 0, and valid centroids. Calculate pairwise Euclidean distances between centroids and apply the gravity model formula: Interaction_ij = (Population_i * Employment_j) / Distance_ij^2.0 for all i != j. Calculate zone interaction potential as Potential_i = sum_j(Interaction_ij) for all j != i. Export the top 20 zone pairs by interaction strength to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_interactions_top20.csv with columns origin_zone, destination_zone, and interaction (sorted descending). Export zone interaction potential data to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_interaction_potential.csv with columns zone_id, population, employment, and interaction_potential (sorted descending). Save a heatmap visualization of the interaction matrix to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/interaction_heatmap.png.
